blocks.text: Scope: Level 0 Context Diagram, Functional Hierarchy,
Level 1 DFD, and Level 2 Process Decompositions

The University Timetable Optimization System (UTOS) is a scheduling system designed to organize university courses, teachers, rooms, constraints, timetable versions, change requests, and reports. The purpose of this document is to present the Data Flow Diagram (DFD) documentation for UTOS, beginning with the Level 0 context diagram and expanding into the functional hierarchy, Level 1 DFD, and selected Level 2 process decompositions.
External actors: Admin, Department Coordinator, Teacher, Student, and Facility Manager.
Major system processes: Manage Master Data, Configure Constraints, Generate Timetable, Handle Change Requests, Publish & View Timetable, and Generate Reports.
Main data stores: D1 Master Data, D2 Constraints & Preferences, D3 Timetable Versions, and D4 Change Requests.
DFD notation follows the Yourdon/DeMarco style: processes, external entities, data stores, and labeled data flows.

rows: A transformation of input data into output data, such as Generate Timetable.
A source or destination outside the system boundary, such as Teacher or Admin.
A persistent store such as D1 Master Data or D3 Timetable Versions.
A named data item moving between entities, processes, and data stores.

Figure 7: Level 2 DFD for Process 6 — Generate Reports

rows: 1.1 Receive Input; 1.2 Manage Courses; 1.3 Manage Teachers; 1.4 Manage Rooms; 1.5 Validate Records
Maintains course, teacher, and room records before they are stored in D1 Master Data.
2.1 Receive Constraint Rules; 2.2 Validate Constraints; 2.3 Store Constraints
Captures and stores hard and soft scheduling constraints from the Department Coordinator.
3.1 Validate Input; 3.2 Build Model; 3.3 Run Solver; 3.4 Score Conflicts; 3.5 Save Timetable
Builds and solves the timetable optimization model and saves the final timetable version.
4.1 Receive Request; 4.2 Validate Request; 4.3 Route for Approval; 4.4 Apply Timetable Update; 4.5 Notify Users
Processes teacher change requests and updates timetable versions when approved.
5.1 Publish Timetable; 5.2 Serve Student View; 5.3 Serve Teacher View
Publishes approved timetables and provides role-specific timetable views.
6.1 Route Request; 6.2 Compile Room Utilization; 6.3 Compile Faculty Workload; 6.4 Compile Conflict Quality Report; 6.5 Consolidate Output
Generates utilization, workload, and conflict/quality reports from stored data.

blocks.text: Balancing ensures that every input and output shown at a higher DFD level is preserved in the next detailed level. The diagrams maintain balance by carrying the same external flows and connected data-store flows into the decomposed processes.

rows: Admin master data, constraints, generate command; generated timetable; conflict report
Processes 1, 2, and 3 with flows to D1, D2, D3, and Admin.
Teacher availability, change request, personal timetable
Processes 4 and 5 with flows through D4 Change Requests and D3 Timetable Versions.
Process 2 and Process 4 with constraint settings and approval decision flows.
Student view request and section timetable
Process 5 Publish & View Timetable with Student output flow.
Facility Manager report request and utilization report
Process 6 Generate Reports with D1 and D3 data access.
Master data input, conflict report, D1 Master Data, master data to Process 3
Flows through 1.1 Receive Input, 1.5 Validate Records, D1 Master Data, and Process 3.
Master data, constraints, generated timetable, conflict report
Flows through 3.1 Validate Input, 3.5 Save Timetable, D3 Timetable Versions, and Admin.
Change request, request record, pending requests, updated timetable, personal timetable
Flows through 4.1 to 4.5, D4 Change Requests, D3 Timetable Versions, and Teacher.
Report request, timetable data, room/usage data, utilization report
Flows through 6.1 to 6.5, D1 Master Data, D3 Timetable Versions, and Facility Manager.
Course records, teacher records, room records, room capacity, equipment information
Manage Master Data, Generate Timetable, Generate Reports
Hard constraints, soft constraints, department preferences, scheduling rules
Generated timetable, published timetable, updated timetable, timetable history
Generate Timetable, Publish & View Timetable, Handle Change Requests, Generate Reports
Teacher change request records, pending requests, validation results, approval status

blocks.text: This DFD documentation describes the major UTOS data flows, external actors, data stores, and decomposed processes. The Level 0 context diagram defines the system boundary, the functional hierarchy shows process decomposition, the Level 1 DFD shows major internal processing, and the Level 2 DFDs explain detailed workflows for key UTOS processes.
